name: DeepSeek-V3.1 (Thinking)
archDisplay: Mixture-of-Experts (671B total / 37B active) — DeepSeekMoE + Multi-head Latent Attention, hybrid think/non-think chat template
note: Thinking mode of the hybrid V3.1 checkpoint; served via deepseek-reasoner, faster than R1-0528 at similar quality
tpsNote: Exceeds a single RTX 4090's 24GB VRAM even at Q4 (~405GB); requires multi-GPU or heavy CPU offload

results.notes: Humanity's Last Exam, text-only subset, Pass@1
BrowseComp (Acc.), Thinking mode, from official HF model card benchmark table
Codeforces-Div1 Rating, Thinking mode, from official HF model card benchmark table
Aider-Polyglot (Acc.), Thinking mode, from official HF model card benchmark table

verificationNotes:
Confirmed real via HF model card + DeepSeek API docs release post (2025-08-21). Same architecture facts as non-thinking variant confirmed. All 6 thinking-mode benchmark rows confirmed verbatim on the HF model card. Deprecated status and pricing confirmed the same way as the non-thinking record.

2026-07-16: Checked all 5 flagged core-benchmark gaps (mmlu, math, humaneval, swe-bench, mmmu) against (1) the official HF model card benchmark table (huggingface.co/deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-V3.1) and DeepSeek's own release post (api-docs.deepseek.com/news/news250821/), (2) Artificial Analysis (artificialanalysis.ai/models/deepseek-v3-1-reasoning) and Epoch AI (epoch.ai/models/deepseek-v3-1), and (3) the sibling deepseek-v3-1-non-thinking.json and deepseek-r1-0528.json records already in this corpus. Findings: raw MMLU, MATH, and HumanEval do not appear anywhere in the official table for either Thinking or Non-Thinking config — DeepSeek replaced them with MMLU-Redux/MMLU-Pro and AIME/HMMT/LiveCodeBench respectively, the same substitution already visible in this family's R1-0528 update (which likewise dropped raw mmlu/math that R1-671B had reported), so this reads as a deliberate reporting-practice shift, not an oversight. SWE-bench Verified (Agent mode) is reported only in the Non-Thinking column of the official table (66.0, already captured on deepseek-v3-1-non-thinking.json) and is explicitly left blank ("—") in the Thinking column, i.e. DeepSeek itself did not run/publish that eval for this exact config. MMMU is absent from the official table entirely and this checkpoint is vision:false/text-only, so a multimodal benchmark would not be expected; Artificial Analysis only names "MMMU-Pro" as an evaluation category with no numeric score surfaced for this model, and Epoch AI's page states outright "No coding benchmarks evaluated," "No math and science benchmarks evaluated," and "No multimodal benchmarks evaluated" for DeepSeek-V3.1, scoring it only on WeirdML/Fiction.liveBench/SimpleBench/ForecastBench. vals.ai has no MMLU benchmark page at all and its SWE-bench Verified leaderboard does not list any DeepSeek-V3.1 variant. Note: DeepSeek-V3.1-Terminus (a distinct, later Sept-2025 checkpoint update, not this Aug-21 V3.1 release) reportedly scores ~72-74 on SWE-bench Verified per third-party writeups, but that number belongs to a different model file and was deliberately not carried over here to avoid conflating checkpoints. All 5 gaps are treated as genuine, deliberate publication absences and left omitted rather than estimated.
